videos.title: 12 Things to Do in San Jose: Costa Rica’s Best Kept Secret
videos.description:
Welcome to San Jose Costa Rica 2023, the perfect destination for your 2023 vacation! In this video, we're going to explore 12 exciting things to do in San Jose Costa Rica 2023. From natural wonders to cultural experiences, San Jose has something for everyone.

First on our list is the Arenal Volcano, one of Costa Rica's most iconic landmarks. We'll also take a dip in the hot springs at Tabacon, where you can relax and unwind surrounded by lush tropical foliage. For a taste of the local cuisine, we'll visit Mercado Central, a bustling market filled with fresh produce, seafood, and traditional dishes.

Next, we'll explore the stunning La Paz Waterfall Gardens, home to a collection of exotic animals and cascading waterfalls. Then we'll visit the San Jose Museum of Art, where you can admire works from local artists and international masters.

For an adrenaline rush, we'll go ziplining through the rainforest canopy, or white water rafting down the Pacuare River. If you're looking for a more relaxed experience, we'll take a stroll through the Botanical Gardens, where you can see over 3,000 plant species from all over the world.

Other top attractions include the National Theater, the Gold Museum, and the Poas Volcano National Park. And of course, no trip to San Jose would be complete without a visit to the beautiful beaches along the Pacific coast.

So whether you're an adventure seeker or a culture lover, San Jose, Costa Rica has something for everyone. Book your trip for 2023 and experience the best of this vibrant and diverse country. Don't miss out on the opportunity to explore San Jose, Costa Rica 2023. #sanjose
tags: ciudad de san jose costa rica 2023
videos.transcript: join us at 10 minutes travel as we present to you 12 exciting things to do in San Jose Costa Rica if you enjoy our videos don't forget to subscribe and hit the notification Bell for our latest updates San Jose Costa Rica is often overlooked as a destination despite its beautiful jungles and beaches many Travelers pass through San Jose on their way to other destinations such as monteverdi however it's worth exploring the city and its attractions before moving on although arriving in a big city can be intimidating this travel video covers all the amazing things to do in San Jose as well as provides information on the city itself trip planning and safety concerns San Jose is the capital and largest city in Costa Rica with a population of over 2 million it serves as the country's Business Center and has a high standard of living and safety for a large Latin American city San Jose has become the sixth most popular city in Latin America for tourism largely due to the influx of visitors from the United States even though the city has a significant urban area it is located in the heart of a tropical rainforest and a brief Drive Beyond its boundaries will reveal the humid forests and exotic Wildlife that are typical of Costa Rica here are the 12 best things to do in San Jose number 12. visit Manuel Antonio National Park if you're a beach Enthusiast don't miss out on the day trip to Manuel Antonio National Park from San Jose it's arguably the best national park in Costa Rica situated in a coastal jungle Paradise with crystal clear waters for swimming numerous hiking trails and opportunities to spot Wildlife such as sloths monkeys iguanas and a variety of birds you can easily book an organized tour from San Jose to reach the park entrance and begin your day's Adventure once there you can walk along a flat and easy hiking trail to reach the beach where you can either relax or continue hiking further this trip is considered one of the top things to do in San Jose and is worth the full day Excursion number 11. San Jose City tour to get acquainted with San Jose taking a city tour is the best option there are various tour companies offering paid tours which can include Hotel pickup and transportation if you prefer a more private experience with lunch included a paid tour may be a better fit however if you're looking for a cheaper option and don't mind walking a free walking tour is available during the walking tour you'll discover historical facts architecture and interesting stories about the city walking tours are an excellent way to quickly navigate the city and find places to revisit number 10. visit the National Theater the Teatro National Costa Rica also known as the National Theater is a highly esteemed structure in San Jose the building boasts opulent decor and numerous marble sculptures and was previously used to entice affluent visitors to the city and Country the theater still operates today offering performances and tours while it is included in the free walking tour of San Jose visitors are recommended to return for a closer look admission to the theater costs 10 USD and it is open Tuesday to Sunday from 9 A.M to 4 P.M the National Theater is situated in the Central Area of San Jose near the Grand Hotel number nine explore National Museum in San Jose tourists can explore various museums that offer insights into the history culture and politics of Costa Rica the national museum is one of the best museums to visit as it showcases exhibits related to archeology anthropology natural history and Costa Rica's past the museum is housed in an Old Fort called Bella Vista Barracks which still Bears bullet holes on its walls therefore it is a must-visit destination for those who want to learn about Costa Rica's Rich history the museum charges eight USD for foreign visitors while students can enter by paying 4 USD it opens daily at 8am and closes at 4pm except on Sunday days when it opens at 9 00 am number 8. explore La Paz waterfall Garden if you're interested in seeing stunning waterfalls during your trip to Costa Rica La Paz waterfall Garden is a must visit destination it's a privately owned property that offers a variety of attractions including natural waterfalls animal enclosures a butterfly garden a bird sanctuary a hummingbird garden and a trout lake the largest animal sanctuary in Costa Rica the property features five breathtaking waterfalls that you can explore along the trail Network the garden is located about an hour and a half drive from San Jose and can be visited independently or as part of a tour that includes transportation and a delicious lunch number seven hike to the giant hand at La chimba coffee Farm I experienced a unique activity during my visit to San Jose which involved a brief walk through a coffee estate to reach a Viewpoint that resembled a human hand the trail named Hacienda y beneficio La chimba spanning over five and nine kilometers at La chimba coffee plantation leads to the Viewpoint with one of the trails being slightly lengthier for those who prefer hiking the trail is easily traceable and despite being a bit muddy and slick in some places it is relatively simple with a gradual upward slope towards the viewpoint we opted for the shorter route to the top and thoroughly enjoyed the experience we arrived early in the morning when the plantation opened at 5 30 a.m and trekked through the estate without encountering anyone else The View was stunning the plantation can be reached from San Jose by taking an Uber and the cost of hiking the trail is 12 USD the trail opens at 5 30 a.m and closes to hikers at 2PM Le chimba offers coffee tours and there is a beautiful restaurant on the premises where visitors can dine while enjoying the company of birds for those seeking an adrenaline rush there is a zip line available number six visit the Museum of Costa Rican art the largest art collections of San Jose and Costa Rica are housed in the museum of Costa Rican art which contains a range of art forms such as sculptures paintings photographs Engravings videos and photos the museum is a blend of indoor and outdoor spaces it is now available free of charge and open for visitors on Tuesday to Sunday from 9am to 4 P.M number five visit a butterfly garden Costa Rica offers a multitude of options for those who wish to visit a butterfly garden this activity is considered to be one of the most popular and renowned activities in the country the tours of the gardens usually commence with an informative video that discusses the life of the Butterflies along with some intriguing facts regarding them it is an ideal opportunity to learn something new afterward one can explore the enclosure filled with butterflies and gain a closer look the guide will provide additional information and identify the various species it is a fascinating experience an Uber ride can be taken to the Garden of your choice or you can join an organized tour that also includes a visit to a coffee plantation making it a perfect option for those interested in both activities number four visit Tortuga Island Tortuga island serves as an ideal day trip from San Jose Costa Rica this petite island is solely accessible by boat and is a perfect destination for everyone offering exceptional diving and snorkeling opportunities to witness a diverse range of aquatic creatures including sharks Dolphins octopus and manta rays furthermore Tortuga Island boasts astonishing beaches hiking trails and dense forests that offer canopy zipline tours visitors can also rent Kayaks paddle boards Sun chairs and umbrellas and purchase food and drinks on the beach the most convenient way to explore Tortuga island is by taking an organized tour from San Jose which includes Transportation lunch snorkeling and a boat ride to the island number three visit the Arenal Volcano and hot springs the Arenal Volcano is a breathtaking site in Costa Rica and although it is not located in San Jose it is easily reachable as a result many people opt for a day trip to a renal from San Jose however it is important to note that the drive to Arenal takes about three hours each way nevertheless with the help of a good tour and guide the journey can be just as interesting and picturesque as the destination itself this tour is a full day adventure offering an array of exciting experiences including zip lining waterfall exploration and soaking in Hot Springs additionally it provides meals and has received exceptional ratings from previous guests number two day trip to the monteverdi cloud forest monteverdi cloud forest is a remarkable Forest that offers Misty views Scenic Lookouts waterfalls and hanging Bridges it is a unique destination that differs greatly from the bustling city of San Jose or the popular Costa Rican beach resorts visitors can expect to spot a variety of wildlife as well as interesting flora and fauna it is possible to stay a few nights in the area similar to the Arenal Volcano with Santa Elena being the closest town surprisingly monteverdi offers numerous enjoyable activities number one head over to Jocko although it's not technically an activity to do in San Jose it's worth mentioning that the town of Jaco is a popular tourist destination for those seeking a beach experience San Jose lacks a beach but Jaco is only a short hour and a half drive away many Travelers spend most of their time in Costa Rica in Jocko which has become a resort town with plenty of beautiful hotels to choose from you can easily visit Jocko on a day trip from San Jose or stay for a few nights to enjoy the beach fully a great holiday in Costa Rica involves both City and Beach experiences so splitting your time between San Jose and Jocko is a great idea additionally there are many fun things to do and see in Jocko you agree with our picks check out these other recent videos from 10 minutes travel and be sure to subscribe and ring the bell to be notified about our latest videos

safetyNotes: Don't miss out on the opportunity to explore San Jose, Costa Rica 2023
recommendations: 12 Things to Do in San Jose: Costa Rica’s Best Kept Secret
Other top attractions include the National Theater, the Gold Museum, and the Poas Volcano National Park
Book your trip for 2023 and experience the best of this vibrant and diverse country

Let Me Plan Your Next Trip for Free ➡️ https://jandatraveladventures.com/your-next-trip?video=ZLQ5Eiy0jrM

Planning a trip to Costa Rica and flying into San Jose’s Juan Santamaría International Airport (SJO)? This video is your ultimate guide to navigating arrivals and departures at SJO! Whether you’re a first-time visitor or a seasoned traveler, this video will help you save time, avoid stress, and make your travel experience smooth and enjoyable.

look around because you'll find yours somewhere with your name written on a plaque and that's how you find your driver just like this they're going to take care of me from this point on and make sure I get exactly to my hotel and have no issue another good part I love about having drivers and gods is this I've been putting up with this big old bag since 4:00 a.m. this morning and guess what he takes it I get in the car and get to start relaxing and enjoying purita so I've made it to my van they're going to put me a van today and of course they imediately hand me water which is awesome and I just get to relax and they're going to take me where I need to go which is actually the presidential Hotel so this is how I enjoy Robin in Costa Rica like this are you excited now to cost R but you just don't want to deal with planning it out look down the description box there's a link to my calendar choose a time and we can have a casual free no obligation chat I can tell you all the details about how I plan trips and what I do and so therefore you too can come enjoy Costa Rica and Pur as Dr free as possible so I'm now in the car with the driver we're headed to the airport I'm leaving Costa Rica but just as I showed you in the how to arrive at the airport here in Costa Rica you need to make sure you watch all the rest of this video where the airport at Costa Rica is userfriendly it's also got some weird quirks to it that if you don't know what you're doing it's going to take your stress level from a two to a nine so make sure you watch this and I'm going to show you how to get through all the quirky parts of the airport here in San [Music] Jose okay we've now made it theoretically the airport it is a traffic jam sitting still not moving and we'll just be on this little Hill waiting for somebody to get out of the way this is the first quirky thing about the airport here in San Jose it doesn't move fast so give yourself plenty of time the exact opposite though is the counters where you check in do not open until 2 hours before your flight so you can't check in so what's going to happen with all these people they're here early they're going to have to hang out just watch the beginning of the crazy okay once you get dropped off you then are going through it doesn't matter which gate you're get in they're all going the same kind of place remembery mentioned that you had to be here a minute you can't check in for 2 hours what happens is that all these areas out here get more and more people as the day goes on and eventually people are just laying on the floor because they're coming in on Multi people's shuttles and buses and there's no way they can check in so they just hang out everywhere once you check in it doesn't get any better they have for going through security a line system system but the problem is is the line system is never big enough so it ends up being a kind of pushing shoving herd system before you get up there you getting this weird little line try to make your way to get where you need to get once you make it through the crazy uh checkin Immigration and Customs you hit the shopping area of the airport and they have done it in space everything that you have seen for sale anywhere in Costa Rica from coffee to shirts to hats it's all here and it's actually really good price they're also doing tons of buy three get one buy two get one all these deals so feel free to load up here and oh yeah the brick coffee which is one of my favorite here in Costa Rica is here in every form and you can taste it they have everything that you can try as far as the drink they have the hot chocolate they have all the nuts covered anything you want you can buy and take home and the prices are actually rather similar to what you find in the rest of the country even at the gr store so feel free to buy at the last minute when you get here for a national airport the main airport in the country this is actually rather small there's only 20 gates one half goes One Way half goes the other in between they've done a very good job of restaurants and space and sitting so let me go show you when they redid a few years ago the San Jose Airport they thought ahead first off the Runway is huge so there's any plane can land here the another thing is that they did that shopping area which I've already showed you which is really cool but also they put in some amazing restaurants that are here so when you're hanging out there are no lounges no lounges which for a business class person that's kind of annoying but they do have H cookie bowl places and great burgers and Shake places and a great pizza place they have a lot of really good food here that you would not expect at an airport and the prices really aren't that bad they also have spread out sea in here which is nice and they have artwork everywhere which I really enjoy all the artwork this is the gate sitting for many of the gates here which is kind of nice to have these comfy couches versus those horrible plastic chairs but then they do have a few of the gates that still have these annoying black plastic chairs they just have the absolute wrong Bend for my back so anybody's listening who designs these they need to make them better for us to sit in around the world now if you found my video about the airport here in San Jose helpful make sure you check out these videos.title: The BEST day trips from San Jose, Costa Rica
videos.description:
GET OUR SAN JOSE TOURS DISCOUNTS HERE: https://mytanfeet.com/san-jose-tours-and-activies/

A list of awesome day trips from San Jose, Costa Rica. Fantastic ideas if you're using San Jose as a home base or if you have a couple free days. These day trips from San Jose can be done on your own if you rented a car or you can book with roundtrip transportation included

COSTA RICA CAR RENTAL DISCOUNT: https://mytanfeet.com/special-deals/get-real-discount-rent-car-costa-rica/
LA PAZ WATERFALL GARDENS PARK TICKET DISCOUNT: https://mytanfeet.com/booking/la-paz-waterfall-gardens-tickets/
TORTUGA ISLAND CRUISE DISCOUNT: https://mytanfeet.com/special-deals/tortuga-island-discount/
AUTHENTIC RAINFOREST TOUR: https://mytanfeet.com/booking/authentic-rainforest-experience-san-jose/
RAINFOREST ADVENTURES BRAULIO CARRILLO DISCOUNT: https://mytanfeet.com/special-deals/rainforest-adventures-costa-rica-discount/

WEBSITE
Costa Rica travel blog: https://www.mytanfeet.com

SOCIAL MEDIA

Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/Mytanfeetcom/
Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/mytanfeet/

Chapters
0:00 INTRODUCTION
0:25 ARENAL VOLCANO + HOT SPRINGS   
1:35 MANUEL ANTONIO NATIONAL PARK 
2:32 POAS VOLCANO NATIONAL PARK
3:32 COFFEE TOUR (DOKA)
4:33 LA PAZ WATERFALL GARDENS NATURE PARK
5:49 PACUARE RIVER WHITE WATER RAFTING CLASS 3 & 4
6:43 ISLA TORTUGA DAY CRUISE
7:35 RAINFOREST ADVENTURES BRAULIO CARRILLO
videos.transcript: hey guys I'm Samantha from my tan feet in this video we are going to show you some day trips that you can take from San Jose San Jose is the capital city of Costa Rica and although it's not a very big city you can stay for a day or two or use it as a home base located centrally in the country you are not far from what Costa Rica is known for such as the rainforest beaches and volcanoes the first day trip on our list in no particular order is the arenal volcano tour Arenal volcano is one of the most well-known volcanoes in Costa Rica due to the destructive 1968 eruption nowadays this area is famous for Outdoor Adventures like hiking and ziplining Wildlife watching nature and hot springs making it one of the best places to visit in Costa Rica this one day Arenal Volcano tour from San Jose first stops in a small town called sarchi to visit in oxcar Factory afterwards you'll head to La Fortuna the closest town to Arenal Volcano for a traditional lunch and then you will visit a nearby private reserve that has an amazing Viewpoint of Arenal volcano and Lake arenal to end the day you'll relax in the natural hot springs and enjoy a delicious dinner since this tour has a lot of driving a day trip to Arenal Volcano from San Jose is best done as a tour so you don't have to worry about driving or figuring out where to go Manuel Antonio has been called one of the most beautiful national parks in the world with its pristine white sand beaches crystal clear waters and Lush rainforest this day trip to Manuel Antonio National Park allows you to enjoy The Marvelous beaches and Hike the rainforest trails to stunning viewpoints being tropical rainforest there's plenty of wildlife around and Manuel Antonio National Park is probably one of the best places in Costa Rica to see a sloth you can also see three different kinds of monkeys in Manuel Antonio in case you want to learn more about the Flora and Fauna of Manuel Antonio you can hire a dedicated natural guide from the entrance of the park so that they can find and point out different animals the third day trip from San Jose on our list is to poas Volcano National Park this National Park is just a one hour drive from San Jose and is home to one of the world's largest volcano craters poas volcano is an active volcano constantly steaming and spewing gas and sometimes even erupting you can visit Poas Volcano National Park on your own by driving there in a rental car taking the public bus or booking a tour in case you are renting a car don't forget to check our Costa Rica car rental discount and I'll leave a link to that in the description below at the national park you'll walk the main trail to the Viewpoint the main crater and hopefully you'll visit on a clear day to see it in all its Glory if you want to walk a little bit more there are other Trails through the cloud forest to a dormant volcano if you book a tour to poas volcano most tour companies will combine it with another activity like waterfalls or a coffee tour which then brings me to the next day trip a coffee tour from San Jose there are several coffee plantations in and around San Jose thanks to its high elevation and fertile soil but one of our favorite coffee tours is at the doka state coffee tour and tour companies will normally combine it with poas Volcano National Park and or La Paz waterfall Gardens the doka estate grows some of Costa Rica's highest quality coffee and on the plantation tour you will see and learn about the entire process to learn how to grow a coffee plant how to pick the cherries how to separate and dry the seeds and then roast them to become the delicious coffee beans that we know today you can also visit their store to purchase some unique Costa Rican souvenirs and coffee directly from them the next trip on our list is the La Paz waterfall Gardens you can visit La paz waterfall Gardens on your own if you have your own transportation or as I mentioned earlier you can book it as a tour as many tour companies will combine the poas volcano and coffee tour with La Paz since they're all relatively close to one another the La Paz waterfall Gardens has an animal sanctuary which is home to many animals such as toucans monkeys and jungle cats the animal sanctuary houses Wildlife which sadly cannot be released back into the wild and visitors can learn more about each animal and get a better understanding as to why conservation and environmentalism are so important you can then have lunch at the restaurant or you can head directly to the star of the Nature Park which is the five waterfalls a well-constructed trail takes you to see five amazing waterfalls deep in the rainforest each with its own name and each with its own Viewpoint in case you want to visit La Paz waterfall Gardens on your own we offer a small discount for Park tickets which I'll link below the next day trip from San Jose on our list is for the adventure lovers white water rafting the Pacuare river as one of the top five rivers in the world for white water rafting this trip cannot be missed if you love Outdoor Adventure we have done this tour many times and it is still to this day one of our very favorite things to do in Costa Rica you'll twist turn and paddle through fast Rapids of the pacuare river and be left in awe of the surrounding pristine rainforest you won't stop smiling or laughing the entire time as you conquer class 3 4 and sometimes four plus Rapids with your rafting team the day trip includes breakfast lunch rafting equipment a guide and transportation from San Jose La Fortuna or Puerto Viejo next up is a day cruise to Isla Tortuga or Tortuga Island this island in the Gulf of nicoya is home to not only one of the whitest sand beaches in Costa Rica but also one of the cleanest beaches in Costa Rica on this day trip you'll depart from the punterenas dock to cruise about one and a half hours through the Gulf of nicoya to the island once you arrive to Isla Tortuga you have about five hours on the island to swim snorkel Banana Boat jet ski hike or simply lay on the beach nap and work on your tan when it's time to leave you'll get back on the boat to cruise through the Gulf of nicoya right around the sunset we also offer a small discount for the tortuga Island day cruise from San Jose which I'll link below the last day trip from San Jose on our list is the rainforest Adventures Braulio Carrillo this Adventure Park is only about a 45 minute drive from San Jose and there you can zipline hike or observe the rainforest canopy on their Aerial Tram as this private park borders one of the most biodiverse national parks in Costa Rica the Braulio Carrillo National Park this area is teeming with Wildlife such as toucans sloths ant eaters hummingbirds tapirs and monkeys you can visit on your own or some tour companies will visit this park as part of a Day Tour we visited this park as part of the authentic rainforest experience which we also have a video for and I'll link that below I hope you guys enjoyed this video on day trips from San Jose if you guys are interested in Booking any of these tours just click on the links below and don't forget that we offer a small discount for many day trips from San Jose thanks for watching I'm Samantha from Mytanfeet and we'll see you guys in the next one

recommendations: The BEST day trips from San Jose, Costa Rica

videos.transcript: welcome to vibrant San Jose Costa Rica this bustling capital city is a treasure Trove of experiences that cater to every type of traveler whether you're a history buff a nature lover or a foodie San J offers an incredible blend of culture adventure and charm today we'll uncover the 10 best things to do and explore in this Central American gem ensuring you make the most out of your visit stay tuned because you'll want to add these to your bucket list number one explore the National Theater of Costa Rica let's start our adventure at the National Theater of Costa Rica a true jewel in the heart of San Jose did you know this theater is over a century old opened in 1897 it's a stunning example of neoclassical architecture dripping in opulence the lavish Interiors feature Italian marble gilded statues and an ornate ceiling Fresco that will take your breath away Pro tip opt for a guided tour to uncover fascinating stories behind its construction funded partly by aack on coffee Costa Rica is beloved export number two stroll through Marcato Central ready for a sensory overload head to Marcato Central the Beating Heart of San Jose since 1880 it's more than a market it's an adventure wander through its Labyrinth th ises and discover everything from fresh produce to ory souvenirs imagine the aroma of freshly ground coffee mingling with the scent of sizzling local dishes speaking of food do not miss trying a cassado a hearty traditional meal served at one of the Market's iconic soz small eateries you'll also find unique items like handcrafted wooden Trinkets and vibrant textiles number three discover pre-colombian gold at the Museo Del Oro explore the Museo Del Oro home to one of Latin America's Finest collections of pre-colombian gold artifacts located beneath the plaza de la Cultura discover intricately designed gold pieces dating back to ad500 showcasing ancient craftsmanship and cultural significance the exhibits provide insights into the societal roles and spiritual beliefs of Costa Rica's indigenous peoples number four wander through the peaceful Park Metropolitan oasa escape the city's hustle and bustle at parking metropolitanas saana often referred to as the lungs of San Jose spread over 72 hectares this sprawling Urban Park is the perfect place to unwind did you know was once an international airport now it's the city's green heart home to the National Stadium and several museums whether you're up for a jog a leisurely stroll or a relaxing picnic number four five visit the spyur butterfly garden let's flutter into the enchanting world of the spyur butterfly garden an oasis just a Stones Throw from San Jose bustling City Center imagine stepping into a lush tropical Haven where vibrant butterflies dance around you it's pure magic home to a dazzling array of butterfly species this Garden is a feast for the eyes and soul fun fact butterflies here play a vital role in pollination making them essential to the ecosystem as you wander the Serene Pathways you'll learn fascinating details about butterfly life cycles and habits number six tour the Jade Museum next we're diving deep into ancient history at the Jade Museum a treasure Trove of pre-colombian Wonders did you know Costa Rica boasts the largest collection of American Jade in the world this museum is a time capsule spread across five captivating floors each brimming with exquisite Jade artifacts the exhibits are more than just beautiful displays they unravel the mysterious ceremonial and mystical significance of Jade to Costa Rica ancient civilizations number seven experience bario Aman's historic charm step into a living history book at Baro Aman San Jose most picturesque and historic neighborhood imagine strolling down streets lined with well-preserved colonial buildings each with its own unique story this District oozes charm with its eclectic mix of art galleries Boutique shops and quink cafes here's a fun fact many of the buildings in bario Aman were once lavish homes of coffee baronss as you wander make sure it pop into Cafe Mundo a beloved spot nestled in an old Victorian Mansion offering delicious coffee and an enchanting atmosphere number eight delva De Costa Rica an art at Museo De Arte cerence art lovers this one's for you the Muse de Arte CoStar is a treasure Trove of creativity housed in a stunning building that was once San Jose Airport terminal it's like stepping into a time machine filled with art inside you'll find a rich collection that spans from Colonial times to contemporary masterpieces one of the most EX exting features is the sculpture garden an enchanting outdoor space where art and nature coexist beautifully number nine explore San Jose vibrant street art scene San Jose streets are a canvas for some of the most captivating and thought-provoking street art in Central America grab your walking shoes and embark on a self-guided tour to discover the city's vibrant murals and graffiti from towering colorful walls to intricate stencil work the artistic Expressions you'll encounter are truly mesmerizing keep an eye out for pieces that reflect Costa Rica culture social issues and the country's natural beauty number 10 check out the Central Park in Metropolitan Cathedral no trip to San Jose would be complete without a visit to Central Park and the Metropolitan Cathedral Central Park is the city's Lively epicenter perfect for people watching and so and local Vibes adjacent to it stands the stunning Metropolitan Cathedral an architectural Marvel brimming with history here's an interesting fact the cathedral's clock tower was a gift from England in the 1800s step inside to admire the beautiful stained glass windows and the Serene ornate interior we hope you enjoyed this video if so please hit the like button below and make sure you subscribe to see our new future videos also please let us know in the comment section what is your favorite place that everyone should visit be blessed and thanks for watching [Music] [Music]
